name: Faro de Formentor — round trip from Alcúdia
name_original: Faro de Formentor — ida y vuelta desde Alcúdia
region_intro: Road cycling around Alcúdia offers a diverse landscape, ranging from the flat, easy routes through natural parks to challenging ascents into the Serra de Tramuntana mountains. The region features extensive coastal roads along the Bay of Pollença and Cala Sant Vicenç, alongside inland paths winding through charming villages and olive groves. This varied terrain provides options for cyclists of all levels, from beginners seeking gentle rides to experienced riders tackling demanding climbs. Best road cycling routes around Alcúdia The most popular road cycling route is Faro de Formentor — round trip from Alcúdia, a 45.2 miles (72.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route features dramatic cliffs and impressive Mediterranean views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Port de Pollença Bay – View of Cala Figuera loop from es Barcarès, a difficult 33.9 miles (54.6 km) path. This route offers scenic coastal vistas along the Bay of Pollença. Local road cyclists also love the Descent to Petra – View of Sineu loop from Alcúdia, a 48.3 miles (77.8 km) trail leading through charming villages and olive groves, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Alcúdia is defined by challenging mountain passes, scenic coastal roads, and flat cycling paths through protected natural areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to advanced climbs. The routes in Alcúdia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 2,300 reviews. More than 90,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcúdia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Alcúdia?
A: Alcúdia offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find flat, easy routes through natural parks like S'Albufera, extensive coastal roads along the Bay of Pollença and Cala Sant Vicenç, and challenging ascents into the spectacular Serra de Tramuntana mountains. This variety caters to all skill levels, from gentle rides to demanding climbs.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Alcúdia area?
A: There are over 90 road cycling routes available around Alcúdia, offering a wide range of options for different abilities and preferences. These include 22 easy routes, 38 moderate routes, and 31 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Alcúdia?
A: Yes, Alcúdia offers 22 easy road cycling routes, many of which are suitable for beginners or families. These often feature flat paths through scenic areas like S'Albufera Natural Park, providing a relaxed cycling experience with beautiful views.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling climbs or routes near Alcúdia?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Alcúdia provides access to demanding climbs. The iconic ascent to Faro de Formentor is a popular choice, known for its dramatic cliffs and Mediterranean views. Other significant climbs in the vicinity include Coll de Femenía, Coll de Sa Batalla, and the route to Lluc Monastery. A challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003207410/faro-de-formentor-round-trip-from-alcudia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro de Formentor — round trip from Alcúdia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 45.2 miles with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcúdia?
A: The mild, temperate climate in Alcúdia makes it suitable for road cycling almost year-round. Spring is particularly ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Autumn also provides excellent conditions, with cooler weather perfect for longer rides.

Q: Are there any scenic coastal road cycling routes in Alcúdia?
A: Absolutely. Alcúdia is renowned for its scenic coastal routes. You can enjoy extensive coastal roads along the Bay of Pollença and Cala Sant Vicenç. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38138684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Port de Pollença Bay – View of Cala Figuera loop from es Barcarès&lt;/a&gt;, which offers magnificent coastal vistas.

Q: What kind of landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Alcúdia?
A: Road cycling routes around Alcúdia often pass by various points of interest. You can explore the historic walled town of Alcúdia itself, or venture towards natural highlights. For instance, the route to Cap Formentor offers dramatic views, and you might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way to Cap Formentor&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5041258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final Curves to Cap de Formentor Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;. Inland routes often wind through charming villages and olive groves.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Alcúdia?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Alcúdia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10838382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Petra – View of Sineu loop from Alcúdia&lt;/a&gt; takes you through charming villages and olive groves, returning to Alcúdia.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Alcúdia?
A: The road cycling routes in Alcúdia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 2,300 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's varied terrain, well-maintained roads, and magnificent landscapes, ranging from coastal vistas to inland routes through charming villages.

Q: Are there routes that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, Alcúdia offers routes that traverse protected natural areas. For instance, you can find flat and easy routes through S'Albufera Natural Park, a significant wetland area ideal for scenic cycling and birdwatching. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6081747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Straight Roads Near Petra – Descent to Petra loop from Protected Area: Estany des Ponts&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with good viewpoints for photography?
A: Alcúdia is rich in stunning viewpoints. Many routes, especially those ascending into the mountains or along the coast, offer breathtaking panoramic views. The Cap des Pinar peninsula, separating the bays of Alcúdia and Port Pollença, and the ascent to Talaia d'Alcúdia, provide excellent opportunities for photography, showcasing both bays and a significant portion of Mallorca's mountains.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes around Alcúdia?
A: Yes, Alcúdia features 38 moderate difficulty road cycling routes, perfect for cyclists looking for a bit more challenge than easy routes but not yet ready for the most demanding climbs. These routes often combine varied terrain, including some rolling hills and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10838382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to Petra – View of Sineu loop from Alcúdia&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
Hirer the Torrent de s'Albufereta flows into the Bahia de Pollenca. The torrent has dammed a lake in front of the mouth, which is now partially silted up and has become a habitat for numerous land and water birds and is now a nature reserve.

The park is the largest wetland in the Balearic Islands and was created around 100,000 years ago.
This “road” now consists mostly of gravel and potholes. It’s pretty dangerous for racing bikes and not without danger on the descent either.

Climbing up the tower is definitely worth it. It's a lot of fun and the view is terrific. Once you have climbed the outer ladder, another small ladder leads up the inside of the tower.
Important: It is not recommended to drive to very high altitude, as there is only a small turnaround at the end of the road, which is guaranteed to be parked in high season. The turning with the car is then no longer possible !!!!!! You can then only drive backwards several hundred meters back, which, given the lack of guardrail and the sloping winding road can be very dangerous even for experienced drivers.
The approach from Port de Pollenca is one of the most beautiful cycling routes on Mallorca.
The lighthouse itself is not particularly spectacular, but the way there and the views that are offered are very worthwhile.

You should visit this place and enjoy the view.

This city is (at least for me) a must visit every Mallorcabesuch. First with the bike up to the chapel Calvari and then down a coffee or a refreshing cold drink. The city is (understandably) very popular and therefore also a bit overcrowded, but what does not make them lose their appeal. If there is not so much going on, it is worthwhile to cruise freely or unplanned through the streets or to stroll.
